what_it_does: Defines a full-viewport HTML page that loads graph-related styles and runtime scripts, mounts a DNA graph view from /registry/graph.json into #mount, injects palette CSS variables, and mounts the Vee dock component.
observations: The page title identifies it as "The Graph — the substrate, projected (v1)".
Inline CSS overrides the generic screen framing so the graph fills the viewport at any size.
Scripts load icon data, icon rendering, organism definitions, and the graph view before calling DNA.mountGraph.
DNA.mountGraph is configured with /registry/graph.json and the #mount container.
A module script imports mountVeeDock and paletteCssVars, injects generated palette CSS into the document head, then mounts the Vee dock.
Comments frame the page as an instrument rendering a substrate graph and integrating the V dock.
standouts: The file is primarily a composition shell that wires existing runtime pieces together.
It deliberately escapes fixed phone-slide sizing in favor of responsive viewport filling.
It combines graph mounting, palette variable injection, and a docked interaction component in one page.
conventions: Uses absolute /surface and /registry paths for runtime assets and data.
Keeps page-specific layout overrides inline in the document head.
Relies on global DNA runtime APIs after ordered non-module script loading.
Uses an ES module only for the Vee dock and palette imports.
Commentary is expressive and architectural rather than purely mechanical.
concerns: The layout override uses !important against body display, which signals tight coupling to shared CSS defaults.
There is no visible local error handling if script loading, graph fetch, or mounting fails.
The page depends on ordered globals from several absolute-path scripts.
notes: Brief this as the full-viewport graph instrument page that wires registry data, graph rendering, palette variables, and the Vee dock into one HTML surface.
questions: What runtime behavior is expected if /registry/graph.json is missing or malformed?
Is the Vee dock required for this graph surface or optional chrome?
Are the inline sizing overrides intended to remain file-local or become a reusable graph layout mode?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file presents a responsive graph instrument rendering /registry/graph.json and adding the V dock/palette integration.
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn the project registry graph into a live reusable visual instrument while visually tying it into the shared palette and docked AI panel system.
novelty: A graph data viewer is framed as an "instrument" and paired with runtime palette injection plus a draggable V-branded dock from a plain HTML composition file.
contribution: Provides a file-local graph surface that keeps the fused system's data-driven graph mount, responsive viewport behavior, shared icon/runtime loading, palette-variable bridge, and docked interaction entry point.
summary_for_embedding: This is a full-viewport HTML graph instrument that loads shared DNA runtimes, renders /registry/graph.json through DNA.mountGraph, injects palette CSS variables, and mounts the Vee dock.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to project the registry graph as an interactive responsive substrate view connected to the shared visual system and dock interaction layer.
